reasoning: France are the clear market and model favorites after a perfect 3-0 group stage, and the field has opened further for them since Paraguay's shock penalty win knocked out Germany, removing a potential Round-of-16 obstacle from their path. Argentina win the runner-up slot on the strength of a Messi-inspired 3-0 group, the easier side of the bracket, and the possibility of a 2022-final rematch, though their smaller, technical side could be vulnerable to the tournament's more physical teams in later rounds. Spain (labored, drew with Cape Verde), England (solid under Tuchel) and Brazil are the next tier, with elite depth but lingering form questions. Morocco are the dark horse: they just stunned the Netherlands on penalties to surge up the board and have the squad and big-game pedigree to make another deep run.
